KPIs vs. OKRs:

KPIs = your dashboard (are we healthy?)
OKRs = your GPS (where are we going?)

KPIs track performance.
OKRs drive change.

If you only use KPIs, you stay busy.
If you rely solely on OKRs, you lack stability.

Strong orgs use both.

images.alt: An AI-generated photograph of a stunning, serene landscape in a highland valley. A clear, winding river flows towards the distance in the center of the frame, its surface reflecting the sky and the surrounding hills. The riverbanks are lined with lush green grass. Rolling, green mountains rise up on both sides of the river, forming a long valley that fades into the hazy distance. Above, a bright blue sky is partially filled with scattered, textured white clouds. The entire scene is brightly sunlit, conveying a sense of natural tranquility and breathtaking beauty. The realism of the generated textures on the water, rocks, and grass is striking.
